full_text:
Project update

08.04.2024

1. Recap

Sample 1: Enriched through negative selection
Did not take off and culture was terminated at d6 
 For the next: 
 - Adjust the seeding density to 1000 live cells/mL
 - Positive isolation instead of negative isolation 
Sample 2: Enriched through positive selection and density adjusted
Although this cultures were looking much better than the previous one, we decided in a previous meeting to stick to the negative selection and adjusted cell density 
 For the next: 
 - Adjust the seeding density to 1000 live cells/mL
 - Negative isolation instead of negative isolation 
 - Media without A-83-01 (TGFb inhibitor)

General cell types

2. Project update: recent results

Speaker notes:
Two previous samples were used for: 

Cell number optimization. (Done, now plating 1000 LIVE cells/uL)
Media optimization. (Removal of the TGFb inhibitor, but maybe add something for fibroblasts and endothelial?) 
Harvesting protocol optimization. (Now doing like in the long term organoids protocol) 

This is the one with everything more or less optimized! So I want to gather extra 2 or 3 using the same protocol and ??timeline???

Characterization rather than validation. - > IF

2. Project update: alternative timeline suggestion

Speaker notes:
Two previous samples were used for: 

Cell number optimization. (Done, now plating 1000 LIVE cells/uL)
Media optimization. (Removal of the TGFb inhibitor, but maybe add something for fibroblasts and endothelial?) 
Harvesting protocol optimization. (Now doing like in the long term organoids protocol) 

This is the one with everything more or less optimized! So I want to gather extra 2 or 3 using the same protocol and ??timeline???

Characterization rather than validation. - > IF

Aim: Characterize the cultures (Composition + organization)

Microscope: Andor DragonFly (5 laser system)

This panel could be a good starting point: 

CK7 -> AF568 (Originally in 555)
CD45 -> AF647
a-SMA -> AF750
Nuclei -> Hoechst 33342 
Viability -> Live-or-Dye™ 488/515

3. Imaging cultures at FIMM BIU
